text: Integrating the philosophy of Christian Religious Education (PAK) into the curriculum of theological colleges is not only academically sound but spiritually essential. Often misunderstood as a secular or confusing discipline, philosophy, when viewed as the pursuit of wisdom, finds its highest expression in alignment with Biblical truth. This study explores the vital role of PAK philosophy in shaping the intellectual and spiritual maturity of theology students, enabling them to cultivate wise thoughts, feelings, and actions. By positioning the Holy Scriptures as the ultimate standard for philosophical inquiry, students can reconcile the relationship between faith and reason. The study concludes that specialized learning in this field is fundamental for building up both the individual student and the wider community, ensuring that theological practice is grounded in a robust, Christ-centered worldview.
